history.question: A manufacturer claims that the average life of a certain type of battery is 1000 hours, with a known standard deviation of 120 hours. A sample of 36 batteries is tested, and the average life of the sample is found to be 960 hours. Using a significance level of 0.05, conduct a one-tailed Z-test to determine whether the manufacturer’s claim can be rejected. State your hypotheses, calculate the Z-value, and provide the conclusion.

A researcher conducted a study on a new medication and found that it reduces symptoms in 80% of patients. If they want to test this new medication against a traditional treatment with known effectiveness of 60% using a significance level of 0.05, conduct a hypothesis test using a one-tailed z-test to determine if the new medication is statistically significantly better than the traditional treatment. Assume the sample size for both groups is 100 patients.
